expectations.text: manifest.json exists with UUID in reverse-DNS format
expectations.evidence: manifest.json present at com.dbroesch.obs.sdPlugin/manifest.json; UUID field is "com.dbroesch.obs" — valid reverse-DNS format.

expectations.text: manifest.json contains all 4 action UUIDs (record, stream, mute, scene)
expectations.evidence: Actions array contains: com.dbroesch.obs.record, com.dbroesch.obs.stream, com.dbroesch.obs.mute, com.dbroesch.obs.scene — all 4 present.

expectations.text: manifest.json has CodePath pointing to bin/plugin.js

expectations.evidence: src/plugin.ts exists; line 25: "await streamDeck.connect()" — called as the last step after registering all actions.
Confirmed: src/actions/record-action.ts, src/actions/stream-action.ts, src/actions/mute-action.ts, src/actions/scene-action.ts — all 4 files present.

expectations.text: Scene action uses setTitle() to show current scene name
expectations.evidence: scene-action.ts line 94: "await action.setTitle(truncate(label, 12))" where label is the scene name (or "> sceneName" when active). Called from updateButton() which is triggered on state changes and onWillAppear.
rollup.config.mjs present at outputs root; configures input as src/plugin.ts and output as com.dbroesch.obs.sdPlugin/bin/plugin.js.

expectations.text: No PKCE OAuth code included (not needed for OBS WebSocket)
expectations.evidence: obs-websocket.ts uses OBS WebSocket v5 native authentication (SHA-256 hash of password+salt+challenge). No PKCE, OAuth tokens, authorization_code flows, or code_verifier/code_challenge found anywhere in the codebase.

expectations.text: OBS WebSocket connection logic is present
expectations.evidence: src/services/obs-websocket.ts implements a full OBS WebSocket v5 client: connect(), handleHello() with SHA-256 auth, handleIdentified() to fetch initial state, handleEvent() for real-time updates, sendRequest() with request/response matching, and scheduleReconnect() for resilience.

suggestions: The ws (WebSocket) npm package is used in obs-websocket.ts but is not listed in package.json dependencies — this would cause a runtime error unless it's expected to be bundled from @elgato/streamdeck's transitive deps.
package.json has no obs-websocket or ws dependency listed explicitly; worth verifying the bundling pipeline resolves ws correctly.
Scene action uses SingletonAction but iterates this.actions — correct pattern for multi-instance support despite the singleton decorator name.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score — all 10 assertions pass. The build is well-structured with clean separation between the WebSocket service layer and Stream Deck action handlers. The OBS WebSocket v5 protocol implementation is complete including SHA-256 authentication, request/response correlation, event subscriptions, and auto-reconnect. The scene action correctly uses setTitle() to display the current scene name on the button.
